{"key_event":"Postwar democratization and General Strike defeat","reasoning":"Civil society remained exceptionally strong through mass suffrage, Labour’s rise, and dense associational life, but union defeats, industrial distress, and the 1927 restrictions offset the 1928 franchise gain. State power fell from the wartime command peak after demobilization and the loss of most of Ireland, yet emergency powers and the expanded social-administrative state kept it overwhelmingly capable.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":9.7,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":9.6}
